zh.lifeSummary: 當Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya於大約1170年出生在西班牙拉 利奧亞納拉時，他的父親Diego López de Haro II19歲，母親Maria Manrique de Lara16歲。他於1214年與Urraca Alfonso de León在西班牙結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1236年10月18日享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya于大约1170年出生在西班牙拉 利奧亞納拉时，他的父亲Diego López de Haro II19岁，母亲Maria Manrique de Lara16岁。他于1214年与Urraca Alfonso de León在西班牙结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1236年10月18日享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Díaz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Rafael, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto.
Spanish (Díaz): patronymic from the medieval personal name Didacus (see Diego ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Diazdeleon
meanings: Spanish: combination of the surname Díaz (see Diaz ) and the name León (see Leon ) with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Diego
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Francisco, Juan, Fernando, Jose, Miguel, Mario, Maximo, Salvador, Aida, Ana, Domingo.

Spanish: from the very common male personal name Diego, which in the Spanish-speaking world is usually taken to be a shortened form of Santiago . However, this may be only a folk etymology. The personal name is found in the Middle Ages in the Latin forms Didacus and Didagus, probably a derivative of Greek didachē ‘doctrine, teaching’. However, in view of the fact that this personal name is unknown outside the Iberian Peninsula, it may actually have a pre-Roman origin.

variants.name: Diez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Fernando, Pedro, Rafael, Roberto, Angel, Eduardo, Mario, Ricardo.
Spanish (Díez): variant of Díaz (see Diaz ).
German: variant of   Dietz  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Haro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Juan, Luis, Salvador, Manuel, Carlos, Jorge, Miguel, Alberto, Francisco, Jaime.
Spanish: habitational name from a place in the province of La Rioja, so called from a North Castilian form of Spanish faro ‘beacon’. Compare De Haro .
In some cases possibly also French: metonymic occupational name for a peddler, from an Old French cry used to hail someone or to attract attention.

variants.name: De Haro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Rafael, Arturo, Jaime, Jesus, Ubaldo, Alfredo, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Gustavo, Juan.
Spanish: habitational name, formed with the preposition de ‘from’, for someone from a place called Haro (see Haro ).

variants.name: Haros
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): probably the plural form of Spanish   Haro  .
Greek: variant of   Charos  .

nameMeanings.name: Lope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Lupus ‘wolf’, probably adopted as a calque of the Germanic name Wolf .

nameMeanings.name: Cabeza
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Guillermo, Mario, Luis, Margarita, Miguel, Orlando, Pedro, Alberto, Aristedes, Arturo, Carlos.

Spanish: from cabeza ‘head’ (from Late Latin capitia), either a nickname for someone with a big head or a topographic name from any of numerous minor places called with this word, which was commonly used to denote a small hill.

nameMeanings.name: Brava
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Asturian-Leonese and Galician (Braña): topographic name from braña ‘humid meadow’ (a word of pre-Roman origin), or a habitational name from any of numerous places called (A) Braña, in Galicia, or (La) Braña, in Asturias (Spain).

Italian (Apulia; also Branà): probably from the Greek surname Vranas (also Branas), which may be derived either from Greek vrania, a term denoting kind of traditional quilt in Thrace, or (South) Slavic vrana ‘crow’ (compare Vrana ).

variants.name: Vrana
meanings: ‘Beautiful object’.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya was born about 1170, in Nájera, La Rioja, Spain, his father, Diego López de Haro II, was 19 and his mother, Maria Manrique de Lara, was 16. He married Urraca Alfonso de León in 1214, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 18 October 1236, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya est né environ 1170, à Nájera, La Rioja, Espagne, son père, Diego López de Haro II, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Maria Manrique de Lara, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Urraca Alfonso de León en 1214, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 18 octobre 1236, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya um 1170 in Nájera, La Rioja, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Diego López de Haro II 19 und seine Mutter Maria Manrique de Lara 16. Er hat 1214 in Spanien Urraca Alfonso de León geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 18. Oktober 1236 im Alter von 67 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya è nato il circa 1170 nel Nájera, La Rioja, Spagna, il suo padre, Diego López de Haro II, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Maria Manrique de Lara, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Urraca Alfonso de León nel 1214, nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 18 ottobre 1236, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcayaが約1170年にスペインラ リオジャンaジャーアで生まれた時，父Diego López de Haro IIは19歳，母Maria Manrique de Laraは16歳であった。1214年にスペインでUrraca Alfonso de Leónと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1236年10月18日に67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya(이)가 대략 1170년 스페인 라 리오자 aj라에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Diego López de Haro II(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Manrique de Lara(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1214년 스페인에서 Urraca Alfonso de León(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1236년 10월 18일 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya nasceu aproximadamente 1170, em Nájera, La Rioja, Espanha, seu pai, Diego López de Haro II, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Maria Manrique de Lara, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Urraca Alfonso de León em 1214, em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 18 de outubro de 1236, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya родился около 1170 г. в местности нaджира, ла риоджа, Испания, его отцу по имени Diego López de Haro II было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Manrique de Lara, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Urraca Alfonso de León в 1214 г. в местности Испания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 18 октября 1236 г. в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lope Cabeza Brava Díaz II de Haro de Vizcaya nació aproximadamente 1170, en Nájera, La Rioja, España, su papá, Diego López de Haro II, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Maria Manrique de Lara, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Urraca Alfonso de León en 1214, en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 18 de octubre de 1236, a la edad de 67 años.
